title: Should You Make Big Decisions Right Now?
summary: There's a common piece of advice to avoid big decisions right after a loss — here's a more useful, nuanced version of it.
category: After a Loss I: The First Weeks
author: HarborCare Circle Editorial
seoTitle: Should You Make Big Decisions Right Now? | HarborCare Circle
metaDescription: There's a common piece of advice to avoid big decisions right after a loss — here's a more useful, nuanced version of it.
whatYouNeedToKnow: Truly optional big decisions (selling a home, major purchases) are usually worth delaying.
Some decisions have real deadlines and can't wait, and that's alright.
The goal is distinguishing genuine urgency from perceived urgency.

paragraphs: Decisions without a real deadline — selling a house, giving away belongings, making major financial moves — are usually worth delaying weeks or months, since grief can affect judgment and perspective in ways that settle with time.

Some decisions do have genuine time constraints (certain financial deadlines, lease decisions), and it's alright to make those when needed — the goal isn't to avoid all decisions, just to tell the difference between what's truly urgent and what only feels urgent right now.

nextSteps: Identify which pending decisions have a real deadline versus which don't.
Delay the ones without a real deadline, even if there's pressure to decide.
Ask a trusted, less emotionally involved person to help sanity-check bigger decisions.
disclaimer: General guidance — a financial advisor or attorney can clarify which specific decisions have real deadlines in your situation.
